title: National Diabetes Prevention Program (National DPP)
section: CDC-Recognized Lifestyle Change Program for Prediabetes
text: The CDC-recognized National Diabetes Prevention Program (National DPP) is a structured year-long lifestyle change program for adults with prediabetes (HbA1c 5.7%-6.4%, fasting plasma glucose 100-125 mg/dL, or 2-hour OGTT 140-199 mg/dL) or a history of gestational diabetes. Goals are 5%-7% body weight loss and at least 150 minutes per week of moderate-intensity physical activity. The program comprises 16 weekly core sessions over the first 6 months followed by monthly maintenance sessions. The Medicare Diabetes Prevention Program (MDPP) covers this benefit for eligible Medicare beneficiaries; many private payers also cover the National DPP. Delivery formats include in-person sites (e.g., YMCA), distance learning, and CDC-recognized digital programs (such as Omada, Noom for Health, and Lark).

title: Physical Activity Guidelines for Adults
section: Physical Activity Guidelines for Americans, 2nd Edition
text: Adults should do at least 150 to 300 minutes a week of moderate-intensity, or 75 to 150 minutes a week of vigorous-intensity, aerobic physical activity, or an equivalent combination, preferably spread throughout the week. Adults should also do muscle-strengthening activities of moderate or greater intensity that involve all major muscle groups on 2 or more days a week. Even short bouts of activity count and additional benefits accrue with more activity, up to about 300 minutes a week of moderate-intensity equivalent.
